• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code

LINCS Signature MAE QC

Michael Tran

2026-04-30

This notebook demonstrates the structure and basic use of the final MultiAssayExperiment containing compound-level LINCS signatures and compound annotations.

Setup

Load the MAE

mae <- readRDS(mae_path)

assay_names <- names(experiments(mae))
signature_se <- experiments(mae)[["signatures"]]
signature_mat <- assay(signature_se)

compound_metadata <- as.data.frame(metadata(mae)$Drug.Metadata) |>
  rownames_to_column("Compound.Key") |>
  as_tibble()

coldata <- as.data.frame(colData(mae)) |>
  as_tibble()

feature_metadata <- as.data.frame(rowData(signature_se)) |>
  as_tibble()

selected_gene_metadata <- as.data.frame(metadata(mae)$Selected.Gene.Metadata) |>
  as_tibble()

mae
## A MultiAssayExperiment object of 1 listed
##  experiment with a user-defined name and respective class.
##  Containing an ExperimentList class object of length 1:
##  [1] signatures: SummarizedExperiment with 188 rows and 20788 columns
## Functionality:
##  experiments() - obtain the ExperimentList instance
##  colData() - the primary/phenotype DataFrame
##  sampleMap() - the sample coordination DataFrame
##  `$`, `[`, `[[` - extract colData columns, subset, or experiment
##  *Format() - convert into a long or wide DataFrame
##  assays() - convert ExperimentList to a SimpleList of matrices
##  exportClass() - save data to flat files

Dataset Overview

feature_type_counts <- feature_metadata |>
  count(LINCS.Feature.type, name = "features", sort = TRUE)

annotation_availability <- compound_metadata |>
  mutate(
    annotation_status = if_else(
      In.AnnotationDB,
      "AnnotationDB enriched",
      "No AnnotationDB match",
      missing = "No AnnotationDB match"
    )
  ) |>
  count(annotation_status, name = "compounds") |>
  mutate(
    fraction = compounds / sum(compounds),
    percent = scales::percent(fraction, accuracy = 0.1),
    label = paste0(
      annotation_status,
      "\n",
      scales::comma(compounds),
      " (",
      percent,
      ")"
    )
  )

assay_summary <- tibble(
  assay = assay_names,
  feature_type = "compound-level LINCS signature coefficients",
  assay_name = vapply(
    assay_names,
    \(assay_name) {
      paste(assayNames(experiments(mae)[[assay_name]]), collapse = ", ")
    },
    character(1)
  ),
  features = vapply(
    assay_names,
    \(assay_name) nrow(experiments(mae)[[assay_name]]),
    integer(1)
  ),
  compounds = vapply(
    assay_names,
    \(assay_name) ncol(experiments(mae)[[assay_name]]),
    integer(1)
  )
)

overview_tbl <- tibble(
  quantity = c(
    "Pipeline ID",
    "Pipeline version",
    "Experiments",
    "Assays",
    "Compounds in colData",
    "Drug metadata records",
    "Signature features",
    "Selected genes represented in metadata",
    "Median source signatures per compound",
    "Maximum source signatures per compound",
    "Compounds with PubChem CID",
    "Compounds without PubChem CID"
  ),
  value = c(
    metadata(mae)$Pipeline$ID,
    metadata(mae)$Pipeline$Version,
    fmt_count(length(experiments(mae))),
    paste(assay_names, collapse = ", "),
    fmt_count(nrow(coldata)),
    fmt_count(nrow(compound_metadata)),
    fmt_count(nrow(signature_mat)),
    fmt_count(nrow(selected_gene_metadata)),
    fmt_count(stats::median(compound_metadata$LINCS.Signature.Count)),
    fmt_count(max(compound_metadata$LINCS.Signature.Count)),
    fmt_count(sum(!is.na(compound_metadata$Pubchem.CID))),
    fmt_count(sum(is.na(compound_metadata$Pubchem.CID)))
  )
)

kable(overview_tbl)
quantity value
Pipeline ID lincs
Pipeline version v2.0.0
Experiments 1
Assays signatures
Compounds in colData 20,788
Drug metadata records 20,788
Signature features 188
Selected genes represented in metadata 978
Median source signatures per compound 5
Maximum source signatures per compound 5,384
Compounds with PubChem CID 20,349
Compounds without PubChem CID 439 
kable(assay_summary)
assay feature_type assay_name features compounds
signatures compound-level LINCS signature coefficients values 188 20788

This MAE contains one assay, signatures. Rows are LINCS signature features and columns are compounds keyed by LINCS.CMap.Name. The object is compound-level: each assay column summarizes the source LINCS signatures associated with one compound rather than one perturbation instance.

Inspect the MAE Structure

The object layout is inspected directly below. The experiment container, assay names, compound-level metadata, sample map, and object-level metadata can all be accessed from the printed structure and the code shown in this section.

mae
## A MultiAssayExperiment object of 1 listed
##  experiment with a user-defined name and respective class.
##  Containing an ExperimentList class object of length 1:
##  [1] signatures: SummarizedExperiment with 188 rows and 20788 columns
## Functionality:
##  experiments() - obtain the ExperimentList instance
##  colData() - the primary/phenotype DataFrame
##  sampleMap() - the sample coordination DataFrame
##  `$`, `[`, `[[` - extract colData columns, subset, or experiment
##  *Format() - convert into a long or wide DataFrame
##  assays() - convert ExperimentList to a SimpleList of matrices
##  exportClass() - save data to flat files
experiments(mae)
## ExperimentList class object of length 1:
##  [1] signatures: SummarizedExperiment with 188 rows and 20788 columns
colnames(colData(mae))
##  [1] "LINCS.CMap.Name"       "Pubchem.CID"           "InChIKey"             
##  [4] "In.AnnotationDB"       "AnnotationDB.Name"     "AnnotationDB.SMILES"  
##  [7] "LINCS.MOA"             "LINCS.Targets"         "LINCS.Aliases"        
## [10] "AnnotationDB.Aliases"  "LINCS.Signature.Count" "LINCS.Gene.Count"     
## [13] "LINCS.Feature.Count"
head(as.data.frame(sampleMap(mae)))
##        assay           primary           colname
## 1 signatures            1-EBIO            1-EBIO
## 2 signatures 1-benzylimidazole 1-benzylimidazole
## 3 signatures    1-monopalmitin    1-monopalmitin
## 4 signatures 1-phenylbiguanide 1-phenylbiguanide
## 5 signatures           10-DEBC           10-DEBC
## 6 signatures             2-BFI             2-BFI
names(metadata(mae))
## [1] "Pipeline"               "Selected.Gene.Metadata" "Drug.Metadata"

The sampleMap is one-to-one for this dataset: both primary and colname are LINCS.CMap.Name, and they point to the same compound columns in the signatures assay.

Compound Metadata

compound_metadata |>
  select(
    LINCS.CMap.Name,
    Pubchem.CID,
    InChIKey,
    In.AnnotationDB,
    AnnotationDB.Name,
    LINCS.MOA,
    LINCS.Targets,
    LINCS.Signature.Count
  ) |>
  slice_head(n = 8) |>
  kable()
LINCS.CMap.Name Pubchem.CID InChIKey In.AnnotationDB AnnotationDB.Name LINCS.MOA LINCS.Targets LINCS.Signature.Count
1-EBIO 82320 CXUCKELNYMZTRT-UHFFFAOYSA-N TRUE 1-Ebio NA NA 1
1-benzylimidazole 77918 KKKDZZRICRFGSD-UHFFFAOYSA-N TRUE 1-Benzylimidazole NA NA 12
1-monopalmitin 14900 QHZLMUACJMDIAE-UHFFFAOYSA-N TRUE 1-Monopalmitin P-glycoprotein inhibitor ABCB1 7
1-phenylbiguanide 4780 CUQCMXFWIMOWRP-UHFFFAOYSA-N TRUE Phenylbiguanide NA NA 7
10-DEBC 10521421 GYBXAGDWMCJZJK-UHFFFAOYSA-N TRUE 4-(2-Chloro-10h-Phenoxazin-10-Yl)-N,N-Diethylbutan-1-Amine Akt inhibitor PIM1 117
2-BFI 3080926 YTJOHEUHUVBKSB-UHFFFAOYSA-N TRUE 2-(2-Benzofuranyl)-2-imidazoline NA NA 1
2-PMDQ 6081682 ONSOQRPYFKDOEB-UHFFFAOYSA-N TRUE NA Adrenergic receptor antagonist ADRA1A 1
2-aminobenzenesulfonamide 72894 YAZSBRQTAHVVGE-UHFFFAOYSA-N TRUE 2-Aminobenzenesulfonamide NA NA 4

The main fields commonly used in downstream analysis are:

  • LINCS.CMap.Name: the compound identifier used as the MAE primary key and as the assay column name.
  • Pubchem.CID: the PubChem compound identifier used to join back to the base harmonized drug dataset.
  • InChIKey: the structure-level identifier retained from source and AnnotationDB processing.
  • In.AnnotationDB: whether the compound mapped to AnnotationDB metadata.
  • AnnotationDB.Name and AnnotationDB.SMILES: the minimal AnnotationDB metadata carried with this dataset.
  • LINCS.MOA, LINCS.Targets, and LINCS.Aliases: source LINCS compound annotations.
  • LINCS.Signature.Count: the number of source LINCS signatures contributing to the compound-level signature.
metadata_completeness <- compound_metadata |>
  summarise(
    across(
      c(
        Pubchem.CID,
        InChIKey,
        AnnotationDB.Name,
        AnnotationDB.SMILES,
        LINCS.MOA,
        LINCS.Targets,
        LINCS.Aliases,
        AnnotationDB.Aliases
      ),
      \(value) sum(is_present(value))
    )
  ) |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = everything(),
    names_to = "field",
    values_to = "present"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    missing = nrow(compound_metadata) - present,
    percent_present = fmt_percent(present / nrow(compound_metadata))
  ) |>
  arrange(desc(present))

kable(metadata_completeness)
field present missing percent_present
InChIKey 20788 0 100.0%
Pubchem.CID 20349 439 97.9%
AnnotationDB.SMILES 20349 439 97.9%
AnnotationDB.Aliases 20349 439 97.9%
AnnotationDB.Name 20261 527 97.5%
LINCS.MOA 2527 18261 12.2%
LINCS.Targets 2527 18261 12.2%
LINCS.Aliases 603 20185 2.9% 
ggplot(
  annotation_availability,
  aes(x = "", y = fraction, fill = annotation_status)
) +
  geom_col(width = 1, color = "white") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = label),
    position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
    lineheight = 0.95
  ) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#4C956C", "#C75C5C")) +
  labs(
    title = "AnnotationDB metadata availability for LINCS compounds",
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme_void()

Signature Coverage

The source LINCS data can contain many signatures for the same compound. The compound-level assay stores one signature column per compound while retaining the number of source signatures in LINCS.Signature.Count.

signature_count_tbl <- compound_metadata |>
  summarise(
    compounds = n(),
    minimum = min(LINCS.Signature.Count),
    q1 = stats::quantile(LINCS.Signature.Count, 0.25),
    median = stats::median(LINCS.Signature.Count),
    mean = mean(LINCS.Signature.Count),
    q3 = stats::quantile(LINCS.Signature.Count, 0.75),
    maximum = max(LINCS.Signature.Count)
  ) |>
  mutate(across(everything(), fmt_number))

kable(signature_count_tbl)
compounds minimum q1 median mean q3 maximum
20,788 1 3 5 23.48 12 5,384 
axis_values <- c(1, 3, 10, 30, 100, 300, 1000, 3000)

ggplot(
  compound_metadata,
  aes(x = log10(LINCS.Signature.Count))
) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 45, fill = "#8EA8C3", color = "white") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = log10(axis_values),
    labels = scales::comma(axis_values)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Source signatures per compound",
    x = "Number of source signatures per compound",
    y = "Number of compounds"
  )

compound_metadata |>
  arrange(desc(LINCS.Signature.Count), LINCS.CMap.Name) |>
  select(
    LINCS.CMap.Name,
    Pubchem.CID,
    In.AnnotationDB,
    LINCS.MOA,
    LINCS.Targets,
    LINCS.Signature.Count
  ) |>
  slice_head(n = 12) |>
  kable()
LINCS.CMap.Name Pubchem.CID In.AnnotationDB LINCS.MOA LINCS.Targets LINCS.Signature.Count
bortezomib 387447 TRUE Proteasome inhibitor|NFKB pathway inhibitor PSMB1|PSMB2|PSMB5 5384
MG-132 462382 TRUE Proteasome inhibitor PSMB1 5301
vorinostat 5311 TRUE HDAC inhibitor HDAC6|HDAC1|HDAC2|HDAC8|HDAC3 2350
wortmannin 5691 TRUE PI3K inhibitor PIK3CA|PIK3CG|PLK1 1349
mitoxantrone 4212 TRUE Topoisomerase inhibitor TOP2A 1302
geldanamycin 5288382 TRUE HSP inhibitor HSP90AA1 1295
sirolimus 5282071 TRUE MTOR inhibitor FKBP1A|MTOR 1246
BRD-A19037878 6376322 TRUE NA NA 1193
PD-0325901 9826528 TRUE MEK inhibitor|MAP kinase inhibitor|Protein kinase inhibitor MAP2K1 1025
dasatinib 3062316 TRUE KIT inhibitor|Src inhibitor|PDGFR inhibitor|Ephrin inhibitor|Abl kinase inhibitor|Tyrosine kinase inhibitor EPHA2|ABL1|FYN|KIT|LCK|LYN|PDGFRB|BCR|SRC|YES1 910
tanespimycin 6505803 TRUE HSP inhibitor HSP90AA1 820
troglitazone 5591 TRUE Insulin sensitizer|PPAR receptor agonist PPARG 786

Compound Annotation Landscape

moa_summary <- compound_metadata |>
  filter(is_present(LINCS.MOA)) |>
  count(LINCS.MOA, name = "compounds", sort = TRUE) |>
  slice_head(n = 15)

ggplot(
  moa_summary,
  aes(
    x = compounds,
    y = reorder(stringr::str_wrap(LINCS.MOA, width = 38), compounds)
  )
) +
  geom_col(fill = "#3B6EA8") +
  labs(
    title = "Most frequent LINCS mechanisms of action",
    x = "Number of compounds",
    y = "Mechanism of action"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.margin = margin(5.5, 10, 5.5, 24)
  )

target_summary <- compound_metadata |>
  filter(is_present(LINCS.Targets)) |>
  count(LINCS.Targets, name = "compounds", sort = TRUE) |>
  slice_head(n = 15)

ggplot(
  target_summary,
  aes(
    x = compounds,
    y = reorder(stringr::str_wrap(LINCS.Targets, width = 38), compounds)
  )
) +
  geom_col(fill = "#6E9F64") +
  labs(
    title = "Most frequent LINCS targets",
    x = "Number of compounds",
    y = "Targets"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.margin = margin(5.5, 10, 5.5, 24)
  )

Signature Assay

feature_metadata |>
  slice_head(n = 12) |>
  kable()
Feature.ID LINCS.Feature.index LINCS.Feature.name LINCS.Feature.type LINCS.Feature.value
pert_time 0 pert_time scalar pert_time
pert_dose 1 pert_dose scalar pert_dose
bead_batch:b1 2 bead_batch:b1 bead_batch b1
bead_batch:b10 3 bead_batch:b10 bead_batch b10
bead_batch:b11 4 bead_batch:b11 bead_batch b11
bead_batch:b12 5 bead_batch:b12 bead_batch b12
bead_batch:b13 6 bead_batch:b13 bead_batch b13
bead_batch:b14 7 bead_batch:b14 bead_batch b14
bead_batch:b15 8 bead_batch:b15 bead_batch b15
bead_batch:b16 9 bead_batch:b16 bead_batch b16
bead_batch:b17 10 bead_batch:b17 bead_batch b17
bead_batch:b18 11 bead_batch:b18 bead_batch b18 
kable(feature_type_counts)
LINCS.Feature.type features
cell_iname 131
bead_batch 55
scalar 2 
ggplot(
  feature_type_counts,
  aes(x = reorder(LINCS.Feature.type, features), y = features)
) +
  geom_col(fill = "#7D5BA6") +
  labs(
    title = "Signature feature groups",
    x = "Feature group",
    y = "Number of features"
  ) +
  coord_flip()

The signatures assay stores numeric coefficients. Most entries are zero, so the next summaries show both overall matrix completeness and non-zero coefficient behavior.

assay_values <- as.vector(signature_mat)
nonzero_values <- assay_values[is.finite(assay_values) & assay_values != 0]

assay_quality_tbl <- tibble(
  quantity = c(
    "Matrix rows",
    "Matrix columns",
    "Matrix entries",
    "Missing entries",
    "Non-finite entries",
    "Zero fraction",
    "Non-zero entries",
    "Minimum coefficient",
    "Median coefficient",
    "Mean coefficient",
    "Maximum coefficient"
  ),
  value = c(
    fmt_count(nrow(signature_mat)),
    fmt_count(ncol(signature_mat)),
    fmt_count(length(assay_values)),
    fmt_count(sum(is.na(assay_values))),
    fmt_count(sum(!is.finite(assay_values))),
    fmt_percent(mean(assay_values == 0, na.rm = TRUE)),
    fmt_count(length(nonzero_values)),
    fmt_number(min(assay_values, na.rm = TRUE), 4),
    fmt_number(stats::median(assay_values, na.rm = TRUE), 4),
    fmt_number(mean(assay_values, na.rm = TRUE), 4),
    fmt_number(max(assay_values, na.rm = TRUE), 4)
  )
)

kable(assay_quality_tbl)
quantity value
Matrix rows 188
Matrix columns 20,788
Matrix entries 3,908,144
Missing entries 0
Non-finite entries 0
Zero fraction 88.1%
Non-zero entries 464,394
Minimum coefficient 0
Median coefficient 0
Mean coefficient 0.0325
Maximum coefficient 1,141.524 
tibble(log10_abs_coefficient = log10(abs(nonzero_values))) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = log10_abs_coefficient)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 70, fill = "#C75C5C", color = "white") +
  labs(
    title = "Non-zero signature coefficient magnitudes",
    x = "log10 absolute coefficient",
    y = "Number of coefficients"
  )

feature_stats <- tibble(
  Feature.ID = rownames(signature_mat),
  Mean = rowMeans(signature_mat),
  SD = apply(signature_mat, 1, stats::sd),
  Zero.Fraction = rowMeans(signature_mat == 0)
) |>
  left_join(feature_metadata, by = "Feature.ID") |>
  arrange(desc(SD))

kable(
  feature_stats |>
    select(
      Feature.ID,
      LINCS.Feature.type,
      LINCS.Feature.value,
      Mean,
      SD,
      Zero.Fraction
    ) |>
    mutate(
      Mean = fmt_number(Mean, 4),
      SD = fmt_number(SD, 4),
      Zero.Fraction = fmt_percent(Zero.Fraction)
    ) |>
    slice_head(n = 15)
)
Feature.ID LINCS.Feature.type LINCS.Feature.value Mean SD Zero.Fraction
bead_batch:b10 bead_batch b10 0.1208 10.2921 49.4%
bead_batch:b11 bead_batch b11 0.0821 7.9373 55.1%
cell_iname:VCAP cell_iname VCAP 0.3594 3.8958 25.3%
cell_iname:PC3 cell_iname PC3 0.3409 3.7719 42.5%
pert_dose scalar pert_dose 0.0984 2.7288 0.0%
bead_batch:b2 bead_batch b2 0.1359 2.4490 38.8%
bead_batch:f2b4 bead_batch f2b4 0.0932 2.4458 79.6%
cell_iname:A375 cell_iname A375 0.3357 1.8535 40.0%
cell_iname:HT29 cell_iname HT29 0.3305 1.8529 43.9%
cell_iname:MCF7 cell_iname MCF7 0.3198 1.8515 43.0%
cell_iname:A549 cell_iname A549 0.3546 1.8499 35.3%
bead_batch:b7 bead_batch b7 0.0313 0.9836 84.7%
bead_batch:f1b6 bead_batch f1b6 0.2540 0.4232 46.9%
cell_iname:HCC515 cell_iname HCC515 0.1602 0.3018 71.8%
cell_iname:HEPG2 cell_iname HEPG2 0.1372 0.2848 76.5% 
top_feature_variability <- feature_stats |>
  slice_head(n = 20)

ggplot(
  top_feature_variability,
  aes(x = SD, y = reorder(Feature.ID, SD))
) +
  geom_col(fill = "#D49A3A") +
  labs(
    title = "Most variable signature features",
    x = "Standard deviation across compounds",
    y = "Feature"
  )

UMAP View

A UMAP of the compound-by-feature matrix provides a compact view of global signature structure. The plot below uses a reproducible sample of compounds and colors points by the number of source signatures contributing to each compound-level column.

set.seed(20260430)

variable_features <- feature_stats |>
  filter(is.finite(SD), SD > 0) |>
  pull(Feature.ID)

umap_sample_n <- min(5000L, ncol(signature_mat))

umap_compounds <- compound_metadata |>
  filter(LINCS.CMap.Name %in% colnames(signature_mat)) |>
  slice_sample(n = umap_sample_n)

umap_signatures <- signature_mat[
  variable_features,
  umap_compounds$LINCS.CMap.Name,
  drop = FALSE
]

umap_input <- scale(t(umap_signatures))
umap_input[is.na(umap_input)] <- 0

umap_layout <- uwot::umap(
  umap_input,
  n_neighbors = 15,
  min_dist = 0.2,
  metric = "cosine",
  n_threads = 1,
  n_sgd_threads = 1,
  verbose = FALSE
)

umap_tbl <- as_tibble(umap_layout, .name_repair = \(x) c("UMAP1", "UMAP2")) |>
  bind_cols(
    umap_compounds |>
      select(
        LINCS.CMap.Name,
        LINCS.Signature.Count,
        In.AnnotationDB
      )
  )

ggplot(
  umap_tbl,
  aes(
    x = UMAP1,
    y = UMAP2,
    color = LINCS.Signature.Count
  )
) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.65, size = 0.8) +
  scale_color_gradientn(
    colors = c("#D8D8D8", "#3B6EA8", "#C75C5C"),
    trans = "log10",
    labels = scales::comma
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "UMAP of sampled LINCS compound signatures",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Compound-level signatures for ",
      scales::comma(nrow(umap_tbl)),
      " sampled compounds."
    ),
    x = "UMAP 1",
    y = "UMAP 2",
    color = "Source\nsignatures"
  )

High-Variance Feature Snapshot

This heatmap shows the most variable signature features across the compounds with the largest number of contributing source signatures. Values are scaled by feature for visualization.

top_heatmap_features <- feature_stats |>
  slice_head(n = 30) |>
  pull(Feature.ID)

top_heatmap_compounds <- compound_metadata |>
  arrange(desc(LINCS.Signature.Count), LINCS.CMap.Name) |>
  slice_head(n = 80) |>
  pull(LINCS.CMap.Name)

heatmap_mat <- signature_mat[
  top_heatmap_features,
  top_heatmap_compounds,
  drop = FALSE
]
heatmap_scaled <- t(scale(t(heatmap_mat)))
heatmap_scaled[!is.finite(heatmap_scaled)] <- 0

heatmap_tbl <- as.data.frame(heatmap_scaled) |>
  rownames_to_column("Feature.ID") |>
  as_tibble() |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -Feature.ID,
    names_to = "LINCS.CMap.Name",
    values_to = "Scaled.Value"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    Feature.ID = factor(Feature.ID, levels = rev(top_heatmap_features)),
    LINCS.CMap.Name = factor(
      LINCS.CMap.Name,
      levels = top_heatmap_compounds
    )
  )

ggplot(
  heatmap_tbl,
  aes(x = LINCS.CMap.Name, y = Feature.ID, fill = Scaled.Value)
) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#3B6EA8",
    mid = "white",
    high = "#C75C5C",
    midpoint = 0
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Scaled high-variance signature features",
    x = "Compounds ordered by source signature count",
    y = "Feature",
    fill = "Scaled\nvalue"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )

Basic Access Patterns

The MAE can be used directly as an analysis-ready compound signature matrix. Most analyses will either use the assay matrix in feature-by-compound form or transpose it to compound-by-feature form for modeling.

example_compound <- compound_metadata |>
  filter(In.AnnotationDB) |>
  arrange(desc(LINCS.Signature.Count), LINCS.CMap.Name) |>
  slice(1) |>
  pull(LINCS.CMap.Name)

example_metadata <- compound_metadata |>
  filter(LINCS.CMap.Name == example_compound) |>
  select(
    LINCS.CMap.Name,
    Pubchem.CID,
    InChIKey,
    AnnotationDB.Name,
    LINCS.MOA,
    LINCS.Targets,
    LINCS.Signature.Count
  )

kable(example_metadata)
LINCS.CMap.Name Pubchem.CID InChIKey AnnotationDB.Name LINCS.MOA LINCS.Targets LINCS.Signature.Count
bortezomib 387447 GXJABQQUPOEUTA-RDJZCZTQSA-N Bortezomib Proteasome inhibitor|NFKB pathway inhibitor PSMB1|PSMB2|PSMB5 5384 
example_signature <- tibble(
  Feature.ID = rownames(signature_mat),
  coefficient = signature_mat[, example_compound]
) |>
  left_join(feature_metadata, by = "Feature.ID") |>
  arrange(desc(abs(coefficient))) |>
  select(
    Feature.ID,
    LINCS.Feature.type,
    LINCS.Feature.value,
    coefficient
  ) |>
  slice_head(n = 12)

kable(example_signature)
Feature.ID LINCS.Feature.type LINCS.Feature.value coefficient
cell_iname:SKES1 cell_iname SKES1 2.903178
cell_iname:MDST8 cell_iname MDST8 2.895725
bead_batch:b43 bead_batch b43 2.693052
cell_iname:SKM1 cell_iname SKM1 2.650970
cell_iname:NALM6 cell_iname NALM6 2.573592
cell_iname:NCIH596 cell_iname NCIH596 2.548687
cell_iname:JHUEM2 cell_iname JHUEM2 2.547565
cell_iname:LOVO cell_iname LOVO 2.503753
cell_iname:YH13 cell_iname YH13 2.486437
cell_iname:SKMEL28 cell_iname SKMEL28 2.481146
cell_iname:NCIH2073 cell_iname NCIH2073 2.477228
cell_iname:TC32 cell_iname TC32 2.458383 
egfr_compounds <- compound_metadata |>
  filter(grepl("EGFR", LINCS.Targets, fixed = TRUE)) |>
  pull(LINCS.CMap.Name)

egfr_signatures <- signature_mat[, egfr_compounds, drop = FALSE]

tibble(
  subset = "Compounds with EGFR in LINCS.Targets",
  compounds = length(egfr_compounds),
  features = nrow(egfr_signatures)
) |>
  kable()
subset compounds features
Compounds with EGFR in LINCS.Targets 36 188 
modeling_matrix <- t(signature_mat)

tibble(
  object = c("feature_by_compound_matrix", "compound_by_feature_matrix"),
  rows = c(nrow(signature_mat), nrow(modeling_matrix)),
  columns = c(ncol(signature_mat), ncol(modeling_matrix))
) |>
  mutate(
    rows = fmt_count(rows),
    columns = fmt_count(columns)
  ) |>
  kable()
object rows columns
feature_by_compound_matrix 188 20,788
compound_by_feature_matrix 20,788 188

The compound-by-feature matrix is typically the convenient orientation for machine learning or clustering workflows, while the feature-by-compound matrix matches the stored SummarizedExperiment assay orientation.
